description_long: This object is a modern, stylized violin with a partially open, minimalist body design, featuring a combination of natural wood and black synthetic materials. The body is asymmetrical, with a prominent lower bout and a cutaway section, giving it a contemporary look compared to traditional violins. The neck and scroll are crafted from a lighter wood, while the fingerboard, tailpiece, and chinrest are rendered in a sleek black material. The tuning pegs are visible at the top, and the bridge is positioned over the body, though the strings are not clearly modeled. The instrument lacks the classic f-holes, instead opting for a more abstract, open structure. This violin would stand out in an urban setting, perhaps used by street performers or in modern music venues.
description: A modern, stylized violin with a partially open black and wood body, minimalist design, and visible tuning pegs and bridge.
description_view_0: The violin is seen from a low angle, highlighting the scroll, neck, and the open, curved lower body with black and wood finishes.
description_view_1: This view shows the full length of the violin, emphasizing the asymmetrical, open body and the sleek black fingerboard and tailpiece.
description_view_2: The violin is viewed from above, focusing on the scroll, tuning pegs, and the narrow neck, with less of the body visible.
description_view_3: A side view reveals the depth of the body, the curvature of the scroll, and the open, modern design of the lower bout.
